name: Postage stamp batches use 65,536 buckets distributed by the first 16 bits of chunk address

links.targetTitle: Swarm stores all data as 4KB content-addressed chunks in a Merkle hash tree

global.title: Postage stamp batches use 65,536 buckets distributed by the first 16 bits of chunk address
global.content:
## Claim

Every postage stamp batch has a fixed structure of **65,536 buckets (2^16)** into which chunks are distributed by the first 16 bits of their address, producing non-uniform effective capacity.

## Evidence

- Bucket depth is fixed at 16 regardless of batch depth
- Each bucket holds `2^(depth - 16)` slots
- Chunks assigned to buckets by the first 16 bits of their chunk address
- **Immutable batches** fail once ANY single bucket fills up — random distribution yields only ~62-87% effective utilization depending on batch depth
- **Mutable batches** never exhaust — when a bucket overflows, the oldest chunk in that bucket is replaced (ideal for feeds)

## Effective utilization table (99.9% confidence)

| Depth | Effective capacity | Utilization |
|------:|-------------------:|------------:|
| 20    | ~680 MB            | ~62.89%     |
| 22    | ~7.7 GB            | ~65.05%     |
| 24    | ~43 GB             | ~68.48%     |
| 28    | ~909 GB            | ~87%+       |

## Why it matters

Pricing by nominal `2^depth * amount` dramatically overestimates usable capacity on shallow batches. Users should size up or use `GET /stamps/{id}/buckets` to monitor hot-bucket exhaustion.
global.description: Each batch has 2^16 buckets, each holding 2^(depth-16) slots. Immutable batches fail when any bucket fills, giving ~62-87% effective utilization.
